steps.title: Factor the radicand
steps.content: $x^2 + 10x + 25$ is a perfect square trinomial because $10 = 2 \cdot 5$ and $25 = 5^2$. So $x^2 + 10x + 25 = (x + 5)^2$, and since $x \gt  0$, $x + 5 \gt  0$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• $(x+5)^4$ — this would come from squaring the radicand, not taking a fourth root. Too big.
  • $(x+5)^2$ — equals the radicand itself, not its fourth root.
  • $(x+5)$ — equals the square root of the radicand, not the fourth root.
  • $(x+5)^{1/2}$ — matches the exponent $2/4 = 1/2$ from the calculation above.
That matches Choice D — $(x + 5)^{1/2}$.
